qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Customer API address update accepts arbitrary address IDs and reassigns records across accounts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an ownership-check failure in the customer address API update path, not the advisory's SSTI in customer profile first_name/last_name rendering.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ACL bypass on product update enables public local file read through downloadable sample_url
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding chains an admin ACL gap with file:// handling in downloadable sample_url, which is unrelated to the advisory's customer-profile name SSTI sink.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifested qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-mqhg-v22x-pqj8 is the customer profile SSTI caused by rendering customer-controlled first_name and last_name in packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Resources/views/customers/account/profile/index.blade.php inside a Vue-interpreted context; the session's qualifying findings instead cover an address-update ownership failure and a downloadable sample_url file-read chain.
evidence: The advisory summary and fix commit 4144931da0014c696f9126132ce44d7cfbdb2761 point to SSTI in packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Resources/views/customers/account/profile/index.blade.php where first_name and last_name were rendered directly and then changed to v-text.

The customer_api_address_idor finding is rooted in PUT /api/customer/addresses/edit/{id} and packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Http/Controllers/API/AddressController.php:update(), where request('id') is trusted and customer_id is rewritten without an ownership check.

The downloadable_sample_url_public_file_read finding is rooted in admin.catalog.products.update plus the public downloadable sample route, with sample_url copied via ProductController::downloadSample(), which is a separate ACL and file-handling flaw rather than customer-profile SSTI.
